DEPENDENCIES.md — the master tracking document. Every dependency has its required-by stage, the version bound, and the reason for that bound. Pending entries are clearly marked so you know what to install before each stage is implemented. Has a version history table and reminder comments for the next update.
requirements.txt — pip-installable, with confirmed packages pinned and pending ones commented out with their stage noted. The DIPY version bound has the bug rationale inline so it's clear why 1.12.0 and not 1.10.
environment.yml — conda spec with channels. PyTorch GPU install is commented out with the correct RTX 3070 / CUDA 12.3 command since that can't go through a standard conda channel.
check_env.py — run this before your first pipeline use. It checks Python version, all packages with correct version comparisons, the DIPY 1.10/1.11 bug warning specifically, PyTorch GPU detection, and all system tools (MRtrix3, FSL, FreeSurfer, ANTs). Exits cleanly with a count of OK/warnings/failures. --strict flag exits 1 on any failure, useful for CI or pre-flight checks.
As each new stage is implemented, the workflow is: update DEPENDENCIES.md first (add the new package), then uncomment it in requirements.txt and environment.yml, and add a check block to check_env.py.
